# terrible_vmware_vmware_folder_template_from_vm (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `datacenter` (String) The name of datacenter in which to operate
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `template_folder` (String) The name of the folder that the new template should be placed in Should be the full folder path, with or without the 'datacenter/vm/' prefix For example 'datacenter name/vm/path/to/folder' or 'path/to/folder'
- `template_name` (String) The name to give to the new template.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `datastore` (String) The name of datastore to use as storage for the template.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `folder_paths_are_absolute` (Boolean) If true, any folder path parameters are treated as absolute paths. If false, modules will try to intelligently determine if the path is absolute or relative. This option is useful when your environment has a complex folder structure. By default, modules will try to intelligently determine if the path is absolute or relative. They may mistakenly prepend the datacenter name or other folder names, and this option can be used to avoid this.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `resource_pool` (String) The resource pool to place the template in.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) If the template should be present or absent
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vm_moid` (String) The MOID of the vm to be used to create the template One of vm_name, vm_moid, vm_uuid is required This parameter is ignored when state is 'absent'
- `vm_name` (String) The name of the vm to be used to create the template One of vm_name, vm_moid, vm_uuid is required This parameter is ignored when state is 'absent'
- `vm_name_match` (String) If using vm_name and multiple VMs have the same name, specify which VM should be selected
- `vm_use_instance_uuid` (Boolean) If true, search by instance UUID instead of BIOS UUID. BIOS UUID may not be unique and may cause errors.
- `vm_uuid` (String) The UUID of the vm to be used to create the template One of vm_name, vm_moid, vm_uuid is required This parameter is ignored when state is 'absent'
- `wait_for_template` (Boolean) If true, the module will wait until the template is created to exit.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
